name: LLM Planner (meta-flow)
description: Implements the cuddlytoddly LLMPlanner.propose() loop as an itsharness flow. Takes a goal, runs clarification, generates a FlowSpec JSON via the planner agent, scrutinises it, and emits a valid FlowSpec that can be loaded into the canvas or submitted to /compile + /run. This is the 'dynamic planning as a meta-flow' strategy: planning itself becomes a visible, editable, versioned itsharness workflow.

goal.description: User goal text (maps to toddly goal node description)

skills_available.description: List of available tool/skill names (maps to LLMPlanner skills_summary)

clarifications.description: Clarification answers (maps to clarification node result)

draft_flowspec.description: LLM-generated FlowSpec JSON (maps to LLMPlanner EVENT_LIST output → translated to FlowSpec)

scrutiny_result.description: Plan scrutiniser verdict (maps to build_plan_scrutinizer_prompt output)

final_flowspec.description: Final validated FlowSpec ready to load into canvas or submit to /compile

agents.backstory: Generates itsharness FlowSpec JSON from a natural language goal. Knows the FlowSpec v0.2.0 schema: node types (input, output, llm_call, tool_invoke, condition, parallel_fork, parallel_join, hitl_breakpoint, memory_read, memory_write, subgraph, transform, agent_role, agent_debate), edge types (direct, conditional), and state_schema conventions. Always responds with valid JSON and nothing else.
agents.goal: Produce a complete, valid FlowSpec v0.2.0 JSON for the given goal. Use the minimum number of nodes needed. Always include input and output nodes. Add hitl_breakpoint for any step requiring human action. Use parallel_fork/join for independent tasks that can run concurrently.

agents.backstory: Reviews FlowSpec drafts for correctness and completeness. Checks edge targets, state field coverage, node type appropriateness, and whether the flow will actually achieve the stated goal.
agents.goal: Produce a detailed scrutiny report identifying any issues with the draft FlowSpec. Be specific — name the node IDs and fields that are wrong.

agents.backstory: Applies scrutiny feedback to improve a draft FlowSpec. Makes targeted corrections without restructuring the whole flow unless necessary.
agents.goal: Produce a corrected FlowSpec that addresses all issues identified by the scrutiniser. Always respond with valid JSON and nothing else.

nodes.label: Clarify planning scope
nodes.prompt:
Before generating a flow plan, I need a few details.

Goal: {{$.state.goal}}
Available tools: {{$.state.skills_available}}

Please answer:
1. Preferred adapter runtime (langgraph / crewai / mastra / microsoft_agent_framework)
2. Does this flow need human-in-the-loop steps? (yes/no — will add hitl_breakpoint nodes)
3. Will tasks run in parallel? (yes/no — will add parallel_fork/join)
4. Should clarification questions be asked at runtime? (yes/no — will add a clarify hitl_breakpoint at flow start)
5. Min tasks (default: 3) and max tasks (default: 8)

config.expected_output: A complete, valid FlowSpec v0.2.0 JSON object.
A JSON scrutiny report listing all issues found, or { "issues": [], "overall": "pass" } if the spec is correct.

nodes.label: Validate FlowSpec schema
nodes.system_prompt: You are a FlowSpec v0.2.0 schema validator. Check the spec against the schema rules. Respond with JSON only.
